mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of housing starts and retail sales drives forward equity returns: when housing starts decline while retail sales remain elevated, it indicates a consumption-driven demand imbalance that eventually triggers inventory corrections, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient in a multivariate regression fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high-divergence and low-divergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight industrial capacity, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals positive supply-side momentum that immediately lifts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference between rising and falling INDPRO months within the specified regime, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate reflects broad-based economic expansion that improves corporate earnings outlooks, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional portfolios rebalance toward growth assets.
failure_condition: The sign of the forward return distribution aligns with the GDPC1 growth direction in less than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, or the mean forward return difference between positive and negative GDP growth months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital suppresses equity risk premiums and forces valuation multiple compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's directional predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between nonfarm payroll growth and the unemployment rate captures labor market friction: when payroll additions slow while unemployment remains sticky, it signals structural labor mismatches that dampen productivity growth and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between PAYEMS changes and UNRATE levels fails to explain more than 2% of forward return variance over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the signal produces positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in consumer retail sales directly translates to improved corporate revenue guidance, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through analyst models.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean when RSAFS increases month-over-month within the specified regime, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year out-of-sample evaluation.
